claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you enquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'd like to help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 18, I need to ask: **Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

(For example, A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs, or other approved courses)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished school and he's working now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. I have a follow-up question: **How many hours per week is Alex working?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He works full-time - it's over 24 hours a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me explain the outcome:

Since Alex is 18 years old, has left education or training, and is working 24 hours or more per week, you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for him. According to the rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training.

Child Benefit normally continues until a child turns 16, or up to age 20 if they remain in approved education or training. However, once a child over 16 leaves education/training and works 24+ hours per week, the entitlement ends.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry to inform you that you're not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex under these circumstances. If Alex's situation changes in the future (for example, if he reduces his working hours and returns to approved education or training), you may want to reassess your eligibility at that time.

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and not in approved education or training. He is working full-time for more than 24 hours per week. According to Child Benefit rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Since Alex meets these criteria, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for him.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old. Alex has finished school, is not in any approved education or training, and is working full-time (over 24 hours per week). Under Child Benefit rules, entitlement ends when a child over 16 leaves education/training and works 24+ hours per week. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and not in approved education or training. He is working full-time for more than 24 hours per week. According to Child Benefit rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Since Alex meets these criteria, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for him.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old. Alex has finished school, is not in any approved education or training, and is working full-time (over 24 hours per week). Under Child Benefit rules, entitlement ends when a child over 16 leaves education/training and works 24+ hours per week. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
